channel.title: PINtPRESS
channel.description: Long-form writing from the PINtPOINT team on beer, pubs, and the tools we build. Postcards from beer cities, guides to crawls and styles, and notes on what's new in the app.

item.title: Humphrey Smith: British Beer's Great Awkward Customer
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Humphrey Smith, the Samuel Smith's beer baron who ran Britain's strangest pub chain by his own private rule, has died aged 81. On cheap pints, dark wood, shire horses, boarded-up villages and the row over an air freshener — and on why the whole thing may become easier without him and also less peculiar.

item.title: 12 Years of Bosko: All 12 UK Venues Pouring Pressure Drop's West Coast IPA on 1 May 2026
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Twelve years of Pressure Drop's iconic West Coast IPA, celebrated 1 May 2026 with simultaneous pours at 12 UK venues. Full launch-venue list with PINtPOINT links and back-story on Bosko.

item.title: Left Field Beer: One of Britain's Best Independent Beer Shops
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Fresh American imports (Trillium, Hop Butcher), European specialists (Arpus, CoolHead, Factory) and championed UK indies (DEYA, Buxton), curated from a single Solihull shop — with the deep-dive on why drinking American craft properly is so expensive over here.

item.title: A Postcard from London: The City That Taught the World to Brew, Then Started Again
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: London is one of the oldest beer cities in the world, but the version you can drink now is barely fifteen years old. The third in the postcard trilogy — a comeback layered over an inheritance.

item.title: A Postcard from Melbourne: The City That Built a Craft-Beer Scene on Bars, Not Breweries
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Companion to the San Diego piece — the structural opposite. Catfish, Local Taphouse, Cookie, Hippo, Boilermaker — a scene that turned curation into a category of its own.

item.title: The Drinker's Guide to Hops: What Each Variety Actually Tastes Like
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: A drinker-first reference to the 30-odd hops you're most likely to meet on a UK or European bar — Cascade, Citra, Mosaic, EKG, Saaz and the rest, organised by a six-category flavour framework.

item.title: A Postcard from San Diego: How a Navy Town Became the IPA Capital of the World
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Karl Strauss in 1989. Stone and Ballast Point in 1996. A hop arms race through the 2000s that perfected the West Coast IPA. A tour of how San Diego quietly became the IPA capital and where to drink its legacy today.

item.title: The Chelmsford Beer Mile: A Live Guide to Essex's Best Craft-Beer Crawl
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Eight specialist venues in a mile — bottle shops, a brewpub, railway arch taprooms — walked in the right order. With live tap lists for every stop, plus a footnote on Baddow Brewery (1798–1927) and how Chelmsford Brew Co broke the century-long quiet.

item.title: What's Wrong With Beer Recommendation Systems (And What PINtPOINT Does Differently)
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: A response to Ethan Haley's Untappd-as-a-recommender analysis and NYC Data Science's Ninkasi project. Three walls rating-based recommenders hit — cold start, popularity bias, geography-blindness — and how PINtDEXTER layers around them.
